rule: The library enables rendering tables from structured POCO (Plain Old CLR Object) data or through the manual initialization of column headers and data rows.
Table presentation is configurable, supporting multiple visual styles (Default, Alternative, Minimal) and specific column alignment settings for data such as numeric values.
Table rows must be added only after columns are defined and must contain a number of items that matches the column count.
Cell content exceeding the maximum width is automatically wrapped into new rows, prioritizing breaks at delimiters to avoid splitting words.
Numeric data types can be right-aligned based on configuration.
Tables can optionally display a total row count in the output.
Table column alignment can be configured for specific data types.
The row count summary is optional and can be disabled through configuration.
Tables can be automatically generated from dictionary-based data structures with keys serving as row headers.

The system must support multiple output styles (standard boxed, Markdown, and Minimal) and provide formatting features such as Unicode-aware column alignment, configurable numeric right-alignment, and a row count footer.
The table must support multiple data input formats including collections of objects, DataTables, nested dictionaries, and multi-dimensional object arrays.
The default output destination for rendered tables is the system standard output stream.
